featureName: BUG-009-002: NormalizeURL chaos R30 (control chars, default ports, trailing dot)

executionHistory.evidence: Parent-expanded chaos-hardening child workflow probe ran NormalizeURL with adversarial inputs (control chars 0x00-0x1F + 0x7F, explicit default ports :80/:443/:21, trailing DNS-root dot). Three findings surfaced — F-CHAOS-R30-001 (HIGH: control chars survive into SourceRef → PG insert failure on NUL, log injection on LF/CR, dedup miss on TAB), F-CHAOS-R30-002 (MEDIUM: default ports not elided → dedup miss), F-CHAOS-R30-003 (MEDIUM: trailing host dot not stripped → dedup miss). None had pre-existing coverage by R16/R24/C17 chaos batches.

Finding-owned planning: created BUG-009-002 folder with spec.md (classification + 3-finding reproduction), design.md (root cause + 3-part fix approach with ordering rationale), scopes.md (single Scope 1 with 3 SCN-BK-FIX-002-NNN Gherkin scenarios + 8-row Test Plan + 7 DoD bullets carrying scenario IDs).

Modified internal/connector/bookmarks/dedup.go: added stripURLControlChars helper (scan-first, alloc-only-if-dirty fast path); inserted stripURLControlChars call at top of NormalizeURL before url.Parse (F-CHAOS-R30-001); replaced inline www. strip with ordered host-canonicalisation block — TrimRight trailing dot → www. strip → port elision via defaultPorts map (http:80/https:443/ftp:21) → IPv6 bracket re-add (F-CHAOS-R30-002, F-CHAOS-R30-003). Signature unchanged. Boundary: dedup.go is the only production file modified.

Added 6 adversarial regression tests to internal/connector/bookmarks/dedup_test.go: TestChaosR30_NormalizeURLStripsControlChars (7 sub-cases), TestChaosR30_NormalizeURLElidesDefaultPorts (8 sub-cases), TestChaosR30_NormalizeURLStripsTrailingDot (6 sub-cases), TestChaosR30_ToRawArtifactsRejectsNULInSourceRef, TestChaosR30_ControlCharVariantsDedup, TestChaosR30_StripURLControlCharsFastPath. `go test -count=1 -run 'ChaosR30' ./internal/connector/bookmarks/` → 6/6 tests PASS (27 sub-case PASS rows). Full package suite `go test ./internal/connector/bookmarks/... -count=1` → ok 0.109s. Adversarial fidelity proof: temporarily reverted stripURLControlChars call → 3 R30 tests FAIL with concrete control-byte offset diagnostics; restored call → all 6 PASS again. Transcript recorded in report.md.

All three F-CHAOS-R30-NNN findings have a fix and a regression test that demonstrably catches the bug if reverted. Dedup contract holds for control-char variants of the same URL. PostgreSQL SourceRef TEXT-column safety verified by TestChaosR30_ToRawArtifactsRejectsNULInSourceRef. No regression in any pre-existing NormalizeURL or bookmarks-package test.

`go vet ./internal/connector/bookmarks/...` — clean (no output). `gofmt -l internal/connector/bookmarks/dedup.go internal/connector/bookmarks/dedup_test.go` — clean (no output). Boundary verified: `git diff --name-only HEAD -- internal/connector/bookmarks/` returns dedup.go and dedup_test.go only. Spec 055 WIP files NOT staged.

Appended Chaos R30 execution-history entry to specs/009-bookmarks-connector/state.json; appended 'Chaos R30 — NormalizeURL hardening' section to specs/009-bookmarks-connector/report.md naming this bug and the three F-CHAOS-R30-NNN findings with Fixed status.
